Adversarial reproduction of p8-002-api-pull-ssrf
==================================================

Method: in-process Go integration test against current tree (main branch,
commit 57653b8e). A test file was temporarily placed at
server/ssrf_adversarial_test.go that:
  1. Spins up a local httptest server simulating an internal target that
     returns HTTP 500 with a body containing "AKIA_STOLEN / STOLEN_SECRET".
  2. Builds a model.Name from "<host>:<port>/ns/model:tag".
  3. Confirms n.IsValid() == true (host is accepted by isValidPart(kindHost)).
  4. Invokes pullModelManifest(ctx, n, &registryOptions{Insecure: true}).
  5. Captures the outbound request on the attacker listener.
  6. Captures the error returned upstream to the API caller.

Command:
  go test -vet=off ./server/ -run TestSSRFAdversarial -v

Observed output:

=== RUN   TestSSRFAdversarial_PullManifestToAttackerHost
    crafted name "127.0.0.1:60663/ns/model:tag" -> host="127.0.0.1:60663" ns="ns" model="model" tag="tag" scheme="https"
    outbound request: GET /v2/ns/model/manifests/tag (Host=127.0.0.1:60663)
    error string returned to /api/pull caller: 500: {"AccessKeyId":"AKIA_STOLEN","SecretAccessKey":"STOLEN_SECRET"}
    wrapped error: pull model manifest: 500: {"AccessKeyId":"AKIA_STOLEN","SecretAccessKey":"STOLEN_SECRET"}
    PASS: attacker host received outbound request AND response body was reflected back to API caller
--- PASS: TestSSRFAdversarial_PullManifestToAttackerHost (0.00s)
PASS
ok  github.com/ollama/ollama/server

Interpretation:
  - Attacker-controlled host (including IP literal per isValidPart(kindHost)
    at types/model/name.go:344-372) is embedded in the outbound URL with
    no destination allowlist, no private/link-local/loopback filter.
  - Insecure:true downgrades the scheme to http at server/images.go:952-954.
  - The response body is surfaced verbatim into the error propagated back to
    the /api/pull caller via fmt.Errorf at server/images.go:921-927 and the
    outer wrapper at server/images.go:622-623.

Caveats:
  - The original finding's raw PoC JSON
      {"name":"169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/default:x",...}
    does NOT parse to a valid model.Name because excess "/" is absorbed into
    the host part which then fails isValidPart (no "/" allowed inside host).
    Attacker has to use a 3-part form "HOST/NS/MODEL:TAG". The HTTP path
    sent is always /v2/{ns}/{model}/manifests/{tag}; attacker does not
    control the URL path suffix. This limits direct IMDSv1 credential
    retrieval (which requires specific path /latest/meta-data/...).
  - However, the same flow is fully unrestricted against any internal HTTP
    service that responds on /v2/*/manifests/* or on any path that yields
    sensitive content under 4xx/5xx (internal container registries, Harbor,
    Nexus, and other docker-distribution v2 endpoints are especially
    vulnerable). Probing, fingerprinting, and response-body leakage for
    private / loopback targets are all confirmed via the reflected error.
